steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question. The goal is to find a quotation from Boateng that explains why she used pointillism in "Exquisite." The answer must include (1) a direct quotation from Boateng and (2) a reason or purpose for choosing pointillism, not just a description of the technique or the painting's subject.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — In painting "Exquisite," Boateng applied pointillism to create what she called an "illusion of movement" within the painting's five dancing women and their twirling skirts.: Directly quotes Boateng's stated purpose — creating an "illusion of movement" — and links that purpose to "Exquisite" and its dancing figures. Satisfies both requirements. ✓
  • Choice B — Pointillism, the technique used in Boateng's "Exquisite," involves the application of small, distinct dots of color.: Defines pointillism and notes Boateng used it, but provides no quotation from Boateng and no reason why she chose it. ✗
  • Choice C — In "Exquisite," Boateng uses a technique that she says involves "extending dabs of color in the subject matter into the background and vice-versa.": Includes a quotation from Boateng, but the quotation describes how the technique works mechanically, not why she chose it for this painting. ✗
  • Choice D: Refers to Boateng's views on Ghanaian women, not her reason for using pointillism. ✗
A is correct.
